title: helianthemum lavandulifolium var. stoechadifolium
intro: Helianthemum lavandulifolium var. stoechadifolium, also known as rockrose, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Helianthemum lavandulifolium var. stoechadifolium include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of rockrose can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
